text: Fifty-four-year-old obese woman with diffuse abdominal pain of more than thirty-six hours duration. No prior history of peptic ulcer disease, but she admits to occasional alcohol intake. Examination revealed generalized abdominal tenderness with leukocytosis of nineteen thousand. CT scan of the abdomen showed perforated duodenal ulcer. She was resuscitated and underwent robotic assisted repair of duodenal ulcer perforation.

Robotic ports are placed, and the peritoneum is assessed, revealing macronodular liver cirrhosis. The surgeon suctions gastrointestinal spillage and fibrinous exudate, followed by gentle blunt dissection and inspection of the hepato-duodenal area to identify the perforation on the anterior wall of the duodenum near the duodenal bulb.

The surgeon uses a 3-0 silk suture to repair the duodenal perforation, placing full-thickness bites approximately 0.5 centimeters from the friable edges, extending from one margin to the other. The suture is now passed to include the posterior duodenal wall.

A patch of viable omentum is mobilized and positioned over the duodenal perforation without tension. The surgeon ties sutures to anchor the omental patch, ensuring the tension is sufficient for stabilization while preserving the omental blood supply.
The surgeon irrigates the peritoneal cavity with a large volume of warm saline, focusing on cleaning the suprahepatic and subhepatic recesses and pelvis to remove any remaining contamination.
